Linux kernel is an OS kernel, a system-level component.
Kernel memory management primarily deals with volatile RAM; persistent memory support via drivers (e.g., NVDIMM) but not a built-in persistent memory.
Kernel does not self-improve autonomously; improvements come from external contributors.
No skill ecosystem for AI agents present in the kernel source.
Kernel operates with low autonomy, reacting to system calls and scheduler decisions.
Kernel implements security policies (e.g., SELinux, AppArmor) but lacks AI governance boundaries.
